name: Sourdough Peasant Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Begin by mixing the sourdough starter, water, sugar, and yeast in a large bowl until combined.
2. Gradually add the bread flour to the mixture and stir until a shaggy dough forms.
3. Cover the bowl with a clean kitchen towel and let it rest at room temperature for 30 minutes.
4. If your sourdough starter is very active, the dough may rise quickly and fill the mixing bowl (Hitachi B101).
5. For an extra sour loaf, use the starter that has been fed two or three days prior. This will result in a shorter, heavier loaf.
6. For a light and fluffy loaf, feed the starter first, then let it sit on the counter for 8 to 10 hours until very foamy before proceeding with the recipe.
7. Form the dough into a medium-sized loaf shape and place it in a lightly greased or parchment-lined bowl.
8. Cover the bowl again and let it rise at room temperature until doubled in size, about 1 to 2 hours.
9. Carefully turn the risen dough onto a lightly floured surface.
10. Shape the dough into a tight ball by folding the edges inward.
11. Place the shaped loaf seam-side down on a parchment-lined baking sheet or in a greased loaf pan.
12. Cover and let it rise again until nearly doubled, about 30 to 45 minutes.
13. Preheat your oven to the highest temperature setting (usually around 450°F/230°C) and place a Dutch oven or heavy pot with lid inside to heat up.
14. Carefully invert the risen loaf into the hot pot, score the top with a sharp knife or razor blade.
15. Cover the pot tightly and bake for 20 minutes.
16. Remove the lid and continue baking for an additional 10-20 minutes until golden brown and crusty.
17. Let the bread cool completely on a wire rack before slicing and serving.
